patches.summary: Tighten extractTrackID to require a 'track' or 'tracking' path segment.
patches.reason: The original fallback returned the last path segment for any URL with >=1 segment, silently producing wrong order IDs for non-tracking URLs (e.g. wolt.com/en/foo/menu -> 'menu'). Return empty instead so the command errors honestly.

patches.summary: url.PathEscape the slug and url.QueryEscape the delivery method when building the per-venue dynamic URL in venues-compare.
patches.reason: menu.go already escapes the slug; venues_compare.go was missing the same call. A slug containing %, +, or other URL-special characters would produce a malformed request URL.

patches.summary: venues-now's --max-eta filter now excludes venues with unknown ETA (EstimateMin == 0).
patches.reason: The original guard 'row.EstimateMin > 0 && row.EstimateMin > maxETA' short-circuited for unknown-ETA venues, silently including them even when the user asked for fast delivery. The command's --help promises 'deliver within --max-eta minutes', so unknown-ETA venues cannot satisfy the contract.

patches.summary: Tighten venues-compare description to match what the dynamic endpoint actually returns.
patches.reason: Original Short said 'Compare open status, ETA window, and delivery configs' but venueCompareRow has no ETA fields — the dynamic endpoint returns next-close/open timestamps, not delivery-time-in-minutes (that lives in /pages/restaurants). Updated Short + Long to advertise what we actually return and point users to venues-now for ETAs.

patches.summary: menu items --category now accepts either the category slug or display name; menuItemRow exposes both category and category_slug.
patches.reason: The flag was labelled 'category slug' but the filter compared display names only, so a user copying a slug from `menu categories` output got zero results. Fixed by tracking both name and slug per item and matching against either form case-insensitively. Verified live with both `--category chow-mein-chow-fen-2` and `--category 'Chow Mein & Chow Fen'`.

patches.summary: venues-now and cuisine-bottleneck now dedupe by venue slug across sections of /v1/pages/restaurants.
patches.reason: Wolt groups the same venue into multiple thematic sections (Featured, Top Rated, cuisine buckets). Without dedup, venues-now could return the same venue multiple times and cuisine-bottleneck would count one venue N times per tag, skewing VenueCount, OpenCount, and AvgETAMin. Verified live against Helsinki: 170 venues returned, 0 duplicate slugs.

patches.summary: venues-compare now fans out per-venue dynamic fetches via cliutil.FanoutRun (default concurrency 4).
patches.reason: Sequential N calls scaled linearly with the slug count. Parallelizing caps end-to-end latency at roughly the slowest single call for typical N <= 4. Source-order output is preserved by FanoutRun's slot-based result ordering.

deferred_to_upstream.summary: AdaptiveLimiter.Wait() has a TOCTOU race: lastRequest is read under the lock, the lock is released, both goroutines sleep, both fire simultaneously.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: internal/cliutil/ratelimit.go is generator-emitted shared code present in every printed CLI in this library. A one-CLI patch here would not help the rest; the fix belongs upstream in cli-printing-press.

deferred_to_upstream.summary: When --latest-only is combined with --since, sync.go silently ignores --since but emits no structured JSON warning for agent consumers running in --json mode.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: internal/cli/sync.go is generator-emitted shared code present in every printed CLI. Adding an sync_warning event for this flag combination belongs in the generator template so the whole catalog gets it consistently, not patched per-CLI. Greptile classified this finding as a diagnostics gap, not a data-loss or behavioral defect.

deferred_to_upstream.summary: Generated endpoint wrappers (pages_list-restaurants-near.go, pages_search.go) gate lat/lon params on != 0.0 instead of 'flag was explicitly set', silently dropping coordinates of exactly 0.0 from the request.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Generator-emitted code present in every printed CLI that has float64 query/body params. Affects equatorial / prime-meridian coordinates. Not user-affecting for Wolt in practice (Wolt operates in Europe, Israel, Japan — none near 0,0) but the generator template should switch to cmd.Flags().Changed() or distinguish a sentinel from a real zero.
